title: Errors that occur in SPEL+ tasks
description:
Errors that occur in SPEL+ tasks

Errors that occur in SPEL+ tasks

When errors occur in SPEL+ tasks, they are automatically sent as an event to VB if there is no error handler.   If you use an error handler in a SPEL+ function, you can signal your VB application that an error has occurred by firing an event.  Catch the error in a SPEL+ error handler and then use a SPELCom_Event statement to fire an event in VB.

Function conveyr

Integer spelErr, evErr

OnErr GoTo eHandler

' processing here

Exit Function

eHandler:

spelErr = ERR(0)

evErr = 10000 + spelErr

SPELCom_Event evErr, ErrMsg$(spelErr)

Fend
examples:
Function conveyr Integer spelErr, evErr OnErr GoTo eHandler ' processing here Exit Function

eHandler: spelErr = ERR(0) evErr = 10000 + spelErr SPELCom_Event evErr, ErrMsg$(spelErr)

Fend
